title: phaius pauciflorus subsp. sabahensis
intro: Phaius pauciflorus subsp. sabahensis is a subspecies of terrestrial orchid native to Borneo, characterized by its unique, solitary flowers and strap-shaped leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Phaius pauciflorus subsp. sabahensis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it may also be used in traditional medicine practices to treat various health conditions.
